mcqs.question: Which step in the ML process typically consumes 60-80% of the project time?
mcqs.explanation: Data preparation (cleaning, EDA, feature engineering, selection) is the most time-consuming step in any ML project.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of splitting data into training, validation, and test sets?

options.text: To increase the total amount of data
To objectively evaluate model performance and tune hyperparameters

mcqs.explanation: Training set trains the model, validation set tunes hyperparameters, and test set provides final unbiased evaluation.

mcqs.question: Which is NOT part of the data preparation step?
mcqs.explanation: Hyperparameter tuning happens during model training/evaluation, not during data preparation.

mcqs.question: Concept drift in ML refers to:

options.text: The statistical properties of the target variable changing over time
The training data being too small

mcqs.explanation: Concept drift occurs when the real-world relationship between inputs and outputs changes, making the deployed model less accurate.

mcqs.question: Which evaluation metric is used for regression problems?
mcqs.explanation: MSE measures average squared difference between predicted and actual values. Accuracy/F1 are for classification; silhouette is for clustering.

mcqs.question: What should be done FIRST in an ML project?

options.text: Collect as much data as possible
Define the problem and success metrics

mcqs.explanation: Problem definition is always the first step — without a clear problem statement, you cannot determine what data to collect or which algorithm to use.
